definition: Commodity supercycles historically coincide with positive stock-bond correlation regimes, with direct implications for 60/40 portfolios. The diversification benefit of commodities depends critically on whether the supercycle is supply-driven (negative equity beta — good hedge) or demand-driven (positive equity beta — poor hedge). The 2022 episode already educated institutional investors, partially correcting the mispricing.
evidence: 1970s persistent positive correlation; 2022 as modern template
Supply-driven vs. demand-driven distinction determines hedging properties
Rolling correlation data confirms regime-dependent behavior
open_questions: Is the current episode supply-driven or demand-driven, and is it mixed?
Has institutional education from 2022 permanently altered positioning behavior?
